card.hook: If a wallet-safety extension trusts the wallet’s rendered signature or analyzes only the proxy shell, documented drainer bypasses and ERC-1967 upgradeable contracts can hide the real consequences of a transaction. The design here argues the verdict has to come from independent decode plus pre-confirmation simulation on the effective implementation contract.
card.why_it_matters: If you are learning to build browser-extension security for smart-contract flows, this gives you a concrete rule for where your checks must run and what they cannot trust.
card.action: Write a 3-sentence note after reading the "Failure mode" section that states the two named ways this system can fail and the single design constraint they imply for your extension.
